page.title: Bipolar Disorder — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Bipolar Disorder: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Bipolar Disorder: 108 alterations and 389 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Association between air pollutants and four major mental disorders.
Adjuvant CoQ10 to a standard medication improved symptoms of depression after 8 weeks of treatment.
Both yoga and psychoeducation may improve residual symptoms of unipolar and bipolar depression as add-on to medications.
Omega-3 fatty acids were shown to be more effective than placebo for depression in both adults and children in small controlled studies and in an open study of bipolar depression.
The addition of bright light therapy might be effective in rapidly ameliorating depressive core symptoms of vulnerable difficult-to-treat depressed outpatients.
The data from this study provide robust evidence that supports the efficacy of midday bright light therapy for bipolar depression.
The findings in this open-label nonrandomized open-label trial suggest efficacy and safety of psilocybin with psychotherapy in BDII depression.
There is a significant negative association between the rate of hospital admissions due to depressive disorders and solar activity.
Psychedelics in the treatment of unipolar and bipolar depression.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Natural episodic pattern: untreated, median episode length 3-6 months; recurrence in 90% within 5 years; functional impairment between episodes in 30-60%; suicide risk 20-30x general population
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Lithium reduces recurrence risk by 30-40% and suicide risk by 60-70% (unique anti-suicidal effect not shared by other mood stabilisers); combination pharmacotherapy + structured psychotherapy (IPSRT, CBT): 60-70% euthymia maintenance at 2 years in first-episode BD
remission.gap_size: Moderate — lithium underuse is a genuine gap; psychotherapy chronically under-integrated with pharmacotherapy
remission.barrier_detail: Lithium is the most evidence-based mood stabiliser with the only demonstrated anti-suicidal effect but is declining in use relative to newer anticonvulsants and antipsychotics partly due to monitoring requirements; many clinicians avoid lithium due to perceived complexity despite its superior evidence base
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — NICE/APA guidelines reaffirm lithium as first-line for BD prevention; 2019 cariprazine and lumateperone approved; atypical antipsychotics (quetiapine, olanzapine, aripiprazole) as mood stabilisers since 2000s; lithium first used 1949
remission.outdated_treatments: Valproate monotherapy in women of childbearing age (teratogenic, superior alternatives available; EU restricted 2018, compliance poor); antidepressant monotherapy in BD (high switch-to-mania risk; not guideline-recommended but frequently prescribed in unrecognised BD); long-term benzodiazepine co-administration
remission.adjunctive_emerging: N-acetylcysteine (NAC) — RCTs showing depressive symptom reduction; omega-3 fatty acids (adjunctive depressive symptom benefit); ketamine/esketamine for acute bipolar depression (off-label, growing evidence); circadian rhythm interventions (social rhythm therapy, light therapy for bipolar depression)
remission.frontier_signal: Agonist-antagonist combination (lurasidone + lithium optimization protocols); personalized pharmacogenomics-guided lithium dosing; psilocybin for bipolar depression (Phase 2 underway with careful mania-risk monitoring); digital phenotyping for mood prediction and early intervention
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
